name: Maryland
tooltip: Maryland — Cat 4: No codified statutory right of publicity. Common-law misappropriation may apply.
rightOfPublicity: Common law only. Maryland has not enacted a codified general right-of-publicity statute. Common-law misappropriation may apply — elements vary, but generally: defendant appropriated plaintiff's identity for commercial value, plaintiff is identifiable, defendant received a benefit. Consult an attorney for state-specific application.
postMortem: No codified post-mortem term. Descendibility uncertain at common law.
aiCoverage: Md. Code, Crim. Law § 3-809 (SB 360, 2025 Ch. 219, signed Apr. 22, 2025, eff. July 1, 2025) — synthetic-image NCII.
remedy: Common-law misappropriation suit. Federal claims available regardless: Lanham Act § 43(a) false endorsement, defamation, and federal NCII statutes apply nationwide.
whatsInteresting: Maryland hasn't built statutory likeness protection. AI-specific statutes exist for narrow areas (election deepfakes, NCII, etc.) but the general right-of-publicity base is common law only.
primarySources: Common-law cases vary by state; consult an attorney licensed in Maryland.
Federal statutes and any state AI-specific statutes listed above.
